mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained reduction in the federal funds rate signals an aggressive monetary easing stance by the central bank, which typically lowers the cost of capital and boosts investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops by more than 0.5 percentage points over the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Central bank easing typically supports asset prices
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS has dropped by more than 0.5pp in 3 months, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A noticeable increase in the unemployment rate indicates a weakening labor market and broader economic contraction, which translates into lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over the last 3 months
failure_condition: If, when UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp in 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing activity and overall supply-side constraints or reduced demand, leading to lower corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident economic indicator
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant negative correlation with the 3-month percentage change in INDPRO over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals a severe contraction in the housing market and broader consumer demand, which negatively impacts construction, financial, and retail sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 (thousands of units) on a 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market downturns often precede economic recessions
failure_condition: If, when HOUST is below 1000 (3-month MA), the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales growth and employment growth decelerate significantly, it indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and income, leading to reduced corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending and employment are key drivers of economic growth
failure_condition: If, during periods where both RSAFS and PAYEMS growth conditions are met, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and can lead to tighter monetary policy, all of which negatively impact corporate margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month percentage change is greater than 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can reduce real corporate earnings
failure_condition: If, when CPIAUCSL 12-month % change > 3.0%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive more than 60% of the time, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive real GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which typically translates into robust corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary driver of equity market performance
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with the year-over-year percentage change in GDPC1 over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong employment growth and a low unemployment rate signifies a robust labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods where both PAYEMS growth and UNRATE conditions are met, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more than 40% of the time, this hypothesis is rejected.
